incident_sources.label: Generic news wire — M&S cyberattack, helpdesk mention (Apr 2025)
incident_sources.text: Marks & Spencer is dealing with a cyberattack. Early reporting suggests the attackers may have talked their way past the company's IT help desk to gain access, though this has not been officially confirmed. The retailer has notified the authorities.

incident_sources.label: Cyber Monitoring Centre — Category 2 classification (May 2025)
incident_sources.incentive_bias: regulator/independent body — floor not full picture
incident_sources.text: The Cyber Monitoring Centre has classified the Marks & Spencer and Co-operative Group cyber events of April–May 2025 as a single Category 2 systemic event, noting social engineering of IT help-desk processes as the disclosed initial access vector.

incident_sources.label: Marks & Spencer Group plc — LSE RNS announcement (22 Apr 2025)
incident_sources.text: Marks & Spencer confirms a cyber incident in which its IT help-desk processes were manipulated, disrupting online order fulfilment and contactless payments. Regulators have been notified. No post-compromise technical detail such as credential-database access is disclosed.
